workflow_name: Internal Account Review Transcript -> Team Actions
user_message: Just came out of migration architecture with Pacific Northwest Outfitters. Turn this into my brief and pull out the asks.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: migration architecture Discussion - Pacific Northwest Outfitters
Date: 2024-01-16
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Andrew Taylor (Pacific Northwest Outfitters), Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft)

[00:02:00] Andrew Taylor (Pacific Northwest Outfitters): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on migration architecture from our last session.

[00:08:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): Our director reviewed this and has concerns about cost optimization. Can we address those?

[00:12:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): On the migration architecture front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around partner engagement.

[00:16:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is partner engagement.

[00:21:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on cost optimization and found that the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades.

[00:26:00] Andrew Taylor (Pacific Northwest Outfitters): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is vendor dependencies.

[00:28:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): Our CTO reviewed this and has concerns about budget approval. Can we address those?

[00:31:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is budget approval.

[00:36:00] Andrew Taylor (Pacific Northwest Outfitters): Our VP reviewed this and has concerns about data residency. Can we address those?

handoffs.condition: team coaching or risk review input

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF52-N trap (Separation of customer-facing draft from internal brief when one unified output ); produces complete Team action items with owners, coaching notes (constructive frame), customer-facing draft (sanitized
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF52-N failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF52-N failure mode (Separation of customer-facing draft from internal brief when one unified output )
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct migration architecture decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF52-N -- Separation of customer-facing draft from internal brief when one unified output
Guardrail axis scored neutrally -- focus on whether output served the user's actual need
